root_text.an5.99:1.4: samantā catuddisaṁ anuviloketvā tikkhattuṁ sīhanādaṁ nadati;
root_text.an5.99:1.6: So hatthissa cepi pahāraṁ deti, sakkaccaññeva pahāraṁ deti, no asakkaccaṁ;
root_text.an5.99:1.7: mahiṁsassa cepi pahāraṁ deti, sakkaccaññeva pahāraṁ deti, no asakkaccaṁ;
root_text.an5.99:1.8: gavassa cepi pahāraṁ deti, sakkaccaññeva pahāraṁ deti, no asakkaccaṁ;
root_text.an5.99:1.9: dīpissa cepi pahāraṁ deti, sakkaccaññeva pahāraṁ deti, no asakkaccaṁ;
root_text.an5.99:1.10: khuddakānañcepi pāṇānaṁ pahāraṁ deti antamaso sasabiḷārānampi, sakkaccaññeva pahāraṁ deti, no asakkaccaṁ.
root_text.an5.99:2.1: Sīhoti kho, bhikkhave, tathāgatassetaṁ adhivacanaṁ arahato sammāsambuddhassa.
root_text.an5.99:2.2: Yaṁ kho, bhikkhave, tathāgato parisāya dhammaṁ deseti, idamassa hoti sīhanādasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.99:2.3: Bhikkhūnañcepi, bhikkhave, tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti, sakkaccaññeva tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti, no asakkaccaṁ;
root_text.an5.99:2.4: bhikkhunīnañcepi, bhikkhave, tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti, sakkaccaññeva tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti, no asakkaccaṁ;
root_text.an5.99:2.5: upāsakānañcepi, bhikkhave, tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti, sakkaccaññeva tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti, no asakkaccaṁ;
root_text.an5.99:2.6: upāsikānañcepi, bhikkhave, tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti, sakkaccaññeva tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti, no asakkaccaṁ;
root_text.an5.99:2.7: puthujjanānañcepi, bhikkhave, tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti antamaso annabhāranesādānampi, sakkaccaññeva tathāgato dhammaṁ deseti, no asakkaccaṁ.

translation_text.an5.99:1.1: “Mendicants, towards evening the lion, king of beasts, emerges from his den,
translation_text.an5.99:1.4: and roars his lion’s roar three times.
translation_text.an5.99:1.5: Then he sets out on the hunt.
translation_text.an5.99:1.6: If he strikes an elephant, he does it carefully, not carelessly.
translation_text.an5.99:1.10: or any smaller creatures—even a hare or a cat—he does it carefully, not carelessly.
translation_text.an5.99:1.12: Thinking: ‘May I not lose my way.’
translation_text.an5.99:2.1: ‘Lion’ is a term for the Realized One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha.
translation_text.an5.99:2.2: When the Realized One teaches Dhamma to an assembly, this is his lion’s roar.
translation_text.an5.99:2.3: When the Realized One teaches the monks …
translation_text.an5.99:2.7: or ordinary people—even food-carriers and hunters—he teaches them carefully, not carelessly.
translation_text.an5.99:2.9: Because the Realized One has respect and reverence for the teaching.”
